message:
Last winter, my family got caught off guard by a surprise ice storm. Nothing dramatic—but I spent 45 minutes hunting for flashlights while my 8-year-old asked "Mom, why is everything dark?" and I realized one flashlight was in the car, another lost its batteries somewhere in 2019, and a third... well, let's just say it was optimistic to think it still worked.

Turns out the comedy of NOT being ready is that it's never actually funny when it's happening. 😅

Now we keep a little ready-to-go bag for the season—batteries that are actually fresh, a real flashlight, water, snacks. Nothing fancy. Just enough that next time a storm rolls through, I can actually *be there* with my family instead of stress-scrambling in the dark.

What's the weirdest place you've ever found a dead battery when you really needed it?
